examples.title: TCP Three-Way Handshake Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how TCP establishes connection using SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK exchange
examples.code:
// TCP Three-Way Handshake
// ========================

// Client Side
BEGIN
 client_seq = generate_random_number()
 send_packet = create_TCP_segment(
 flags = SYN,
 sequence_number = client_seq
 )
 send(send_packet, to_server)
 state = SYN_SENT
 
 // Wait for response
 receive(response)
 IF response.flags == SYN-ACK THEN
 server_seq = response.sequence_number
 client_ack = server_seq + 1
 
 send_packet = create_TCP_segment(
 flags = ACK,
 sequence_number = client_seq + 1,
 acknowledgment_number = client_ack
 )
 send(send_packet, to_server)
 state = ESTABLISHED
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: Client initiates connection by sending SYN with a random sequence number. Server responds with SYN-ACK. Client confirms with ACK. This establishes a reliable bidirectional connection.

examples.title: DHCP Process Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates the DORA process for automatic IP address assignment
examples.code:
// DHCP DORA Process
// ====================

// Client Side - DHCP Discover
FUNCTION dhcp_discover()
 broadcast(DHCP_DISCOVER, 
 transaction_id = generate_id(),
 hardware_address = get_mac_address())
END FUNCTION

// Server Side - DHCP Offer
FUNCTION dhcp_offer(discover_packet)
 available_ip = get_available_ip()
 broadcast(DHCP_OFFER,
 offered_ip = available_ip,
 transaction_id = discover_packet.transaction_id)
END FUNCTION

// Client Side - DHCP Request
FUNCTION dhcp_request(offered_ip)
 broadcast(DHCP_REQUEST,
 requested_ip = offered_ip,
 transaction_id = generate_id())
END FUNCTION

// Server Side - DHCP Acknowledge
FUNCTION dhcp_acknowledge(request_packet)
 assign_ip(request_packet.requested_ip, 
 request_packet.client_mac)
 broadcast(DHCP_ACK,
 allocated_ip = request_packet.requested_ip,
 subnet_mask = 255.255.255.0,
 gateway = 192.168.1.1)
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: DHCP uses broadcast messages to assign IPs. Client broadcasts Discover, Server offers IP, Client requests that IP, Server acknowledges. Uses UDP ports 67 and 68.

examples.title: DNS Query Resolution
examples.description: Demonstrates how DNS resolves domain names to IP addresses
examples.code:
// DNS Query Resolution
// ======================

FUNCTION resolve_domain(domain_name)
 // Check local cache first
 IF cache.contains(domain_name) THEN
 RETURN cache.get(domain_name)
 END IF
 
 // Query DNS server
 dns_server = get_dns_server() // Usually from DHCP
 query = create_dns_query(
 domain = domain_name,
 record_type = A // IPv4 address
 )
 
 send(query, to = dns_server, port = 53)
 response = receive()
 
 IF response.has_records() THEN
 ip_address = response.get_first_A_record()
 cache.add(domain_name, ip_address)
 RETURN ip_address
 ELSE
 RETURN ERROR // Domain not found
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example: resolve_domain("www.google.com")
// Returns: 142.250.190.46 (example)
examples.explanation: DNS resolves human-readable domain names to IP addresses. Client queries DNS server (typically UDP port 53). Server returns A record (IPv4) or AAAA record (IPv6).
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average with cache, O(n) worst case
